title: been cooking this project for 20 months here’s what the backtests look like
author: Ordinary_Mode1011
selftext:
been working non stop on this project called [signalforge ](https://signalforge.io)for 20 months and today i just want to share a bit of the backtest side you set up buy and sell conditions hit run and it gives you detailed results all server side it goes beyond just showing equity curves you get regime analysis showing how it performs in different market conditions drawdown analysis so you know how deep the dips go and time analysis that tells you when the strategy actually works  
and you can check the Backtestresults here:  
  
[https://signalforge.io/backtest-result/9e7c7178-c162-4d9e-9067-32785ebe4643](https://signalforge.io/backtest-result/9e7c7178-c162-4d9e-9067-32785ebe4643)  
  
i really want your feedback on this what do you think and what should i add i’m also planning to launch a free beta soon if you want to try it out check the [waitlist](https://signalforge.io/waitlist).

comments.author: MobileZombie8236
comments.body: cap, this is just a basic AI made website with mock data. what does your ai trading engine use?

replies.author: Ordinary_Mode1011
replies.body: yeah nothing here is ai it’s all straight math the engine just runs your buy and sell conditions through historical market data server side and then breaks it down with stuff like regime analysis drawdown analysis and time analysis the goal is to actually verify if a strategy holds up instead of just trusting screenshots

replies.author: MobileZombie8236
replies.body: I am sceptical, because your last post you said 18 months and now your saying 20 months hahaha. be honest did you create this website and setup using ai? or something, why lie?

replies.author: Ordinary_Mode1011
replies.body: yeah i just miscalculated the months it’s all real work nothing ai mockups, if you can build something better go ahead i don’t feel i need to prove anything here.

replies.author: Ordinary_Mode1011
replies.body: good catch when tp and sl land in the same candle the system can’t really know which one got hit first so i just flag those trades and leave them out of the final calc better to be transparent than fake precision i’ll try to fix it in future versions by drilling down into lower timeframes so it knows which one actually triggered first
